g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about Passing File Descriptors in Unix/Linux systems - a key IPC technique for inter-process communication.
A file descriptor is a small non-negative integer that processes use to access files, pipes, sockets, and I/O resources in Unix systems.
Simply sending the integer FD value between processes doesn't work because each process has its own separate file descriptor table.
File descriptor passing uses Unix domain sockets with ancillary data to transfer kernel file descriptions between processes.
g.text: 3. Mechanism: Unix Domain Sockets + Ancillary Data
Control Messages use SCM_RIGHTS to carry file descriptors

g.data-narration: The msghdr structure contains msg_iov for data and msg_control for ancillary data like file descriptors.
The cmsghdr structure contains cmsg_type (SCM_RIGHTS for FDs), cmsg_len, and cmsg_data holding the actual file descriptors.
First, create a Unix domain socket using socket(AF_UNIX, SOCK_STREAM, 0) to establish the communication channel.
Bind the socket to a file path using sockaddr_un structure, which creates a socket file in the filesystem.
The sender process connects to the receiver's socket using connect() to establish the communication channel.
g.text: After connect, both processes have connected socket pair

g.data-narration: Before sending an FD, the sender must first open the file they want to share using open() system call.
g.text: 9. Step 4: Sender Opens the File to Share

g.data-narration: Set up the cmsghdr structure with SCM_RIGHTS type and place the file descriptor in the control message data area.
g.text: 10. Step 5: Prepare Control Message with FD

g.data-narration: Use sendmsg() to send the control message containing the file descriptor to the connected process.
The receiver uses recvmsg() to receive the control message and extracts the file descriptor from CMSG_DATA.
g.text: 12. Step 7: Receiver Gets FD via recvmsg()

g.data-narration: Both processes now have file descriptors pointing to the same kernel file description. Reading from either will see the same data.
g.text: 13. Result: Both Processes Share Same File
Note: Different FD numbers, but same kernel file description - reads are shared!

g.data-narration: FD passing is used for Unix socketpair passing in web servers, privilege dropping, and zero-copy data sharing between processes.
Remember the key functions: socket(AF_UNIX), bind, connect, sendmsg with SCM_RIGHTS, and recvmsg to receive the file descriptor.
g.text: All use SCM_RIGHTS in cmsghdr to pass file descriptors

g.data-narration: Important: Always close the original FD after passing if you don't need it. The kernel maintains reference counting on file descriptions.
g.text: Close original FD after transfer if not needed
Kernel uses reference counting - FD lives until both close
